answer:
Yeah, so I remember this one time when I realized a friend of mine was lying to me, and honestly, it changed the way I think about friendships. It started out pretty small, like she told me she couldn't come to my birthday party because she was “sick,” but then I saw her on social media hanging out with other people that same night. At first, I was kind of confused and thought maybe there was an explanation, you know? But as I thought about it more, it started to feel like a breach of trust, and I couldn't really ignore it.

I mean, after that happened, our relationship was definitely not the same. It’s awkward when you know someone isn’t being honest with you—it creates this invisible barrier, and communication just gets way more complicated. I tried to ask her about it in a casual way, but she avoided the topic, which made it even worse. Eventually, we drifted apart, which was tough because we’d been friends for several years.

Looking back, I realize how important transparency and authenticity are in any relationship. That experience taught me to be more discerning about who I trust, and to value honesty above just being polite or trying to avoid conflict. Now, I’m much more proactive about setting boundaries and addressing things directly when something feels off. Honestly, it’s helped me develop stronger, more dependable friendships, and I think that’s a skill that'll be really valuable as I go to university and meet new people. So while it was a difficult lesson, I feel like it’s made me a better friend, overall.
title: Share a time when you knew a friend was lying to you. How did that experience affect the relationship? What did you learn from the experience that will help you in other friendships?
